aliases: அஸாம் மீன் கறி நோன்யா (Assam Meen Curry Nonya)

tamil: அஸாம் மீன் கறி நோன்யா (Assam Meen Curry Nonya)

region_of_origin: Singapore and Malacca, Straits Chinese Peranakan
food_subgroup: Tamarind-based fish curry without coconut milk — whole fish steaks or fillets of Spanish mackerel with okra, tomato, and pineapple

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Nonya Assam Fish Curry. No exact URI available. Closest parent is a generic fish curry dish concept. Null URI retained pending FoodOn ontology extension.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fish, cooked — used as closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for a prepared fish curry dish. No specific Peranakan fish curry code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Nonya Assam Fish Curry. A0CKP (cooked fish) is the closest defensible parent. Composite dish facets would be required for full representation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Nonya Assam Fish Curry or Peranakan fish curry dishes.

preparation_method: rempah fried then braised in tamarind broth

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Nonya Assam Fish Curry is approximately 350g, comprising roughly 200g of Spanish mackerel fish steak, 100g of tamarind-based gravy, and 50g of mixed vegetables including pineapple, okra, and tomato.

nutrient_source.title: Assam Fish Curry Nonya Style — HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient data with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component derivation for micronutrients
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / Foodo.sg; USDA Agricultural Research Service FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macronutrients (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, sugar) sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Assam Fish Curry. Micronutrients (vitamins, minerals, selenium, phosphorus, zinc) derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for Spanish mackerel, tamarind, okra, tomato, and pineapple in proportions consistent with the standard recipe. All values are per 100g of the prepared dish as served.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fish curry and tamarind-based dishes — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for fish-based dishes and tamarind-acidified preparations; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nonya Assam Fish Curry. GI is estimated at 35 (low) based on: (1) the dish is protein-dominant with minimal carbohydrate (4.8g/100g); (2) tamarind organic acids (tartaric acid) are well-documented to reduce gastric emptying rate and blunt postprandial glucose response; (3) analogous fish curry dishes in the University of Sydney GI Database cluster in the low GI range (30–45). Glycemic load per 350g serving = (35 × 4.8 × 3.5) / 100 = 5.9g, rounded to 1.7 per 100g basis. GL per serving = (35 × 16.8) / 100 ≈ 5.9, classified as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 280mg/100g from belacan (shrimp paste); partially offset by potassium 290mg/100g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be mindful of the belacan-derived sodium content (~280mg/100g; ~980mg per 350g serving), though the favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio and absence of coconut milk make this a significantly better choice than coconut milk-based fish curries; portion control and requesting reduced belacan when dining out is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~35), high protein (10.8g/100g), tamarind organic acids slow gastric emptying
type2_diabetes.guidance: Nonya Assam Fish Curry is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes: the high protein content from Spanish mackerel improves satiety and insulin sensitivity, the tamarind-derived tartaric acid blunts postprandial glucose response, and the low glycemic load per serving (~5.9g) makes this one of the most diabetes-friendly Singaporean fish dishes when consumed without rice or with a small portion of low-GI rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: No coconut milk; low saturated fat (0.8g/100g); omega-3 EPA+DHA from Spanish mackerel; anti-inflammatory spice rempah
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Nonya Assam Fish Curry is cardioprotective relative to coconut milk-based curries: the complete absence of coconut milk eliminates the saturated fat burden, Spanish mackerel provides meaningful EPA+DHA omega-3 fatty acids (~0.8–1.0g per 200g fish serving) which reduce triglycerides and inflammation, and the rempah components (turmeric curcumin, lemongrass citral) have documented anti-inflammatory properties; this dish is appropriate for a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Omega-3 from Spanish mackerel; anti-inflammatory turmeric, lemongrass; vitamin C 18mg/100g; selenium 28mcg/100g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Highly beneficial for rheumatoid arthritis: Spanish mackerel provides EPA+DHA omega-3 with established anti-inflammatory effects on joint inflammation, turmeric curcumin and lemongrass citral further suppress pro-inflammatory cytokines, and the selenium content (28mcg/100g) provides antioxidant support relevant to autoimmune joint disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nonya Assam Fish Curry (娘惹亚参鱼咖喱; 亚参鱼) is a signature dish of Peranakan (Straits Chinese) cuisine, originating in Singapore and Malacca. It is fundamentally distinguished from all other Singaporean and Southeast Asian fish curries by the complete and deliberate absence of coconut milk — making it dramatically lower in fat, saturated fat, and calories than Malay, Indian, or Thai fish curries. The dish is built on a sour, pungent tamarind-based gravy: 'assam' in Malay means tamarind (Tamarindus indica), and the tartaric acid in tamarind provides the dish's defining sour acidity and vibrant orange-red colour. The rempah (spice paste) is prepared by grinding dried chilli, shallots, fresh turmeric, lemongrass, and belacan (fermented shrimp paste), then frying until fragrant before tamarind water is added. Spanish mackerel (ikan tenggiri) steaks are braised in the tamarind gravy until just cooked. Pineapple adds a sweet-sour counterpoint; okra thickens the gravy slightly with its natural mucilage; tomato provides freshness and additional acidity. The result is a light, intensely flavoured dish with a complex sour-spicy-umami profile and a silky, slightly viscous gravy. Nutritionally, Assam Fish Curry is one of the most favourable Nonya dishes: 85 kcal/100g, 10.8g protein, only 3.5g fat, 0.8g saturated fat, and 4.8g carbohydrate. Spanish mackerel contributes meaningful EPA+DHA omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin B12 (1.8mcg/100g), selenium (28mcg/100g), niacin (5.5mg/100g), and vitamin D (3.5mcg/100g). The tamarind-acid environment produces a low glycemic index (~35) and low glycemic load per serving (~5.9). The anti-inflammatory rempah components — turmeric (curcumin), lemongrass (citral) — add clinical value beyond basic nutrition. Halal-compatible provided belacan is halal-certified. Gluten-free and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: is assam fish curry good for diabetes
assam fish curry vs coconut milk curry calories

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database, which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. Micronutrients cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for Spanish mackerel, tamarind, okra, tomato, and pineapple. Both are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy. The dish is well-documented in the HPB database and the nutrient profile is consistent with the known composition of the ingredients.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the prepared Nonya Assam Fish Curry dish is not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database; component-level USDA data for Spanish mackerel exists but cannot be reliably apportioned to the composite dish without introducing unacceptable estimation error. water_g set to -1: moisture content of the prepared dish as served is not reported in HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy for this composite preparation. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero): no hydrogenated fats are used in this preparation; confirmed by ingredient composition. GI value of 35 is an analogous estimate — no direct GI measurement for Nonya Assam Fish Curry exists in the University of Sydney GI Database; estimate is based on protein-dominant composition, tamarind organic acid gastric-emptying effect, and clustering of analogous fish curry dishes in the low GI range (30–45) in the Sydney GI Database. Halal status is conditional on use of halal-certified belacan. FoodEx2 code A0CKP (cooked fish) used as closest defensible parent; no specific Peranakan fish curry code exists. Vitamin A value (28 mcg RAE/100g) is expressed as RAE equivalents derived from beta-carotene in turmeric, tomato, and pineapple components.
